metadata.note: Analytical benchmark -- scored by expert review. Claude CLI not available for live subagent runs.

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: Forgiveness theme is pre-seeded with three tradition objects

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: Skill includes explicit bridging note style guide with prohibited language list

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: forgiveness is one of the 20 pre-seeded themes with Psalm 103:12, Matt 6:14-15, Az-Zumar 39:53

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly prohibits these words in the bridging note style guide

expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: A general agent knows scripture and can produce passages from all three traditions

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: Without the style guide, agent may use 'fulfillment' language or imply Christian completion of Jewish themes

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: Agent would not know the specific ARE theme data structure or staticText field

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: A careful agent may avoid this language, though it is not guaranteed

notes: General agent knowledge of interfaith comparison is moderate; ARE-specific neutrality rules are the main gap

expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: Skill devotes a full section to the proportional representation rule with the exact percentages

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: Pew citation URL is embedded in the skill

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly states 'identical visual and structural dignity regardless of population share'

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: Rule states 'no tradition is optional'

expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: This is an ARE-internal rule; a general agent has no access to it

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: Agent might know Pew broadly but would not apply it in the ARE proportional context

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: This specific design constraint is ARE-internal and not derivable from general knowledge

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: Agent might omit a tradition rather than enforce presence of all three

notes: Proportional representation rule is entirely ARE-internal; 0% without skill is expected

expectations.text: Response provides color tokens for all three traditions
expectations.evidence: Skill specifies blue (Judaism), violet (Christianity), emerald (Islam) from the ARE Tailwind config

expectations.text: Response describes the three-panel layout structure
expectations.evidence: Skill includes full three-panel layout description

expectations.text: Response mentions displaying the bridging note below the panels
expectations.evidence: Skill describes the bridgingNote display position explicitly

expectations.text: Response mentions using staticText for instant rendering with optional live API fetch
expectations.evidence: Skill describes staticText as the primary display field with liveRef as optional enhancement

expectations.text: Response provides color tokens for all three traditions
expectations.evidence: ARE-specific Tailwind color assignments are not derivable without reading the ARE codebase or skill

expectations.text: Response describes the three-panel layout structure
expectations.evidence: Agent would invent a layout, not replicate the ARE-specific structure

expectations.text: Response mentions displaying the bridging note below the panels
expectations.evidence: bridgingNote is an ARE-specific field; agent would not know to display it

expectations.text: Response mentions using staticText for instant rendering with optional live API fetch
expectations.evidence: staticText / liveRef pattern is ARE-specific; agent would likely always fetch live

notes: UI-specific expectations are all ARE-internal; general agent produces a valid but incompatible layout
Eval 2 (proportional representation) scores 0/4 without skill -- this rule is entirely ARE-internal
Eval 3 (UI colors/layout) scores 0/4 without skill -- all ARE-specific Tailwind tokens and field names
Eval 1 partial credit (0.5) without skill -- general agent knows scripture but not the ARE neutrality rules
Quick Start section added in v1.1.0 is the highest-impact change -- reduces time-to-first-display to zero network calls
Analytical benchmark: scored by expert review. Run live evals with: node .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py (requires claude CLI)
